//: redb.Identity.Http module configuration template (Tsak Layer 4: {Module}.config.json).
This file ships inside the .tpkg and provides the HTTP transport defaults for the
Identity HTTP facade. Phase 8: the facade runs in its OWN RouteContext
('identity.http') and exchanges with redb.Identity.Core ('identity') exclusively
through `direct-vm://identity-*` brokered messages whose DTOs live in
redb.Identity.Contracts. The two modules no longer share a context or DI.
Override any value via the standard Tsak 5-layer pipeline:
Layer 1 (host appsettings): Tsak:Contexts:default:IdentityTransport:*
Layer 2 (per-context):      Tsak:Contexts:{contextName}:IdentityTransport:*
Layer 3 (context.json):     same shape as below, merged before this file
Layer 4 (THIS file):        defaults shipped with the .tpkg
Layer 5 (Override, wins):   Tsak:Contexts:{contextName}:Override:IdentityTransport:*
Environment-variable overrides use the standard .NET IConfiguration mapping, e.g.:
Tsak__Contexts__identity__Override__IdentityTransport__Http__PublicPort=8443
Tsak__Contexts__identity__Override__IdentityTransport__Http__CorsEnabled=true
PRODUCTION CHECKLIST (per docs/IDENTITY_DEPLOYMENT.md):
• Bind PublicPort behind a TLS-terminating reverse proxy (nginx/HAProxy/ALB).
• Set ManagementPort to a separate value (e.g. 8081) and firewall it off from
the public internet — the management API is admin-only and never exposes CORS.
• Keep CorsRegisteredClientOriginsEnabled=true so onboarding new SPAs via the
management API automatically grants them CORS access — no config redeploys.
• Use AdditionalAllowedOrigins ONLY for dev/staging fallbacks that have no
OAuth client of their own. NEVER set it to '*'.
//RedbInstanceName: Phase 9b: identifies the named IRedbService the HTTP facade uses to back the
shared cluster-wide DataProtection key-ring. MUST point at the SAME database as
redb.Identity.Core's RedbInstanceName so cookies issued by either context decrypt
with either. The HTTP facade has no project-reference on Core — the only thing it
shares with Core is the DP key-ring (rows in the PROPS store) plus the application
//Redb: Named IRedbService instances. Tsak's RegisterNamedRedbServices reads this section
after context.json/{Module}.config.json merge and registers each entry under
'redb-factory:{name}' in the route-context registry. The HTTP facade's child
container resolves the factory whose name matches RedbInstanceName above.
PRODUCTION: keep ConnectionString out of source control — supply via Layer-5:
DEPLOYMENT TIP: place a shared 'context.json' (Layer 3) in the same Libs/ folder as
redb.Identity.Core.tpkg + redb.Identity.Http.tpkg containing only the Redb section;
both .tpkg will pick it up via Tsak's per-folder context.json merge, eliminating

//: PublicPort       — OIDC protocol + discovery endpoints (token, userinfo, .well-known/*).
ManagementPort   — admin API (/api/v1/identity/*, /scim/*). Null = share PublicPort.
Production strongly recommends a separate, firewalled port.
CorsEnabled      — master switch. Required for SPA / native PKCE clients calling
token / userinfo / revocation from a browser context.
Discovery (/.well-known/*) always uses '*' when CorsEnabled=true;
browser-redirect endpoints (authorize, login, logout-form) and
the management API NEVER receive CORS headers regardless.
CorsRegisteredClientOriginsEnabled — when true (default), the resolver allows any
browser Origin matching a registered OAuth application's redirect
or post-logout URI. New SPAs onboarded via the management API
gain CORS access without any config change.
AdditionalAllowedOrigins — CSV of origins always allowed (dev/staging fallbacks).
Per-request matched and echoed as a single origin (browsers reject
CSV in Access-Control-Allow-Origin).
EnableDeviceCodeFlow — mounts /connect/deviceauthorization and /connect/device/verify.
Must also be enabled in Identity:EnableDeviceCodeFlow.
FederationCallbackPath — path used for IdP redirect-back when EnableFederation=true.
Http.//ManagementPort: Production: separate port for admin API, e.g. 8081 (firewalled).
Http.//AdditionalAllowedOrigins: CSV of origins (no trailing slash). Example: 'https://app.dev.local,https://staging.example.com'. NEVER use '*'.
